title: tragia plumieri
intro: Tragia plumieri, also known as the poison nut or poison top, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its toxic seeds and medicinal properties. The seeds of the plant contain a toxic compound called ricin, which has been used in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Tragia plumieri have been utilized to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, offering benefits such as reducing inflammation and relieving pain. In herbal preparations, the seeds are often used in tinctures or infusions, although caution is advised due to their toxicity and potential for adverse effects.
